description_long: This object is a small, vertically oriented obelisk with a stepped, tiered top and a slightly flared base. The surface appears to be made of weathered stone or metal, exhibiting a muted, pale greenish-gray patina. The shaft is rectangular and tapers gently towards the base, while the top features three distinct horizontal ridges, giving it a stylized, almost mechanical appearance. A small circular hole is visible on one side, possibly for mounting or attachment. The overall form is compact and geometric, with clean lines and minimal ornamentation. The object is shown next to a black-and-white scale bar, indicating its modest size.
description: A small, stepped obelisk with a flared base and ridged top, made of weathered greenish-gray material. It features a geometric, minimalist design with a side hole.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full stepped top, rectangular shaft, and flared base.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the depth of the ridges and a circular hole near the upper section.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the stepped top and the tapering shaft, with minimal surface detail.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the stepped top and the side hole, with the base slightly visible.
attribute_base_design: The base is slightly flared and rectangular, with a flat bottom and no visible decorative features. It appears solid and stable, matching the material and finish of the shaft.
attribute_tip_design: The tip consists of three horizontal, stepped ridges stacked atop the shaft, forming a stylized, tiered crown. The finish is consistent with the rest of the object, with no additional decoration.
